steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.perfect: Perfect 2nd plural would be 'flevistis' — 'fletis' is present
hints.future: Future 2nd plural would be 'flebitis' — 'fletis' is present
hints.imperfect: Imperfect 2nd plural would be 'flebatis' — 'fletis' is present

hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are principal parts of 'fugio' (to flee)
hints.floreo, florere, florui, —: These are principal parts of 'floreo' (to bloom/flourish)
hints.flecto, flectere, flexi, flexum: These are principal parts of 'flecto' (to bend)

hints.interrogative (who?): 'qui' can be interrogative, but here it introduces a relative clause modifying 'beati'
hints.conjunction (because): 'quia' means 'because' — 'qui' here is a relative pronoun
hints.indefinite (someone): 'quis' is indefinite — 'qui' here is the relative pronoun 'who'

hints.accusative: Accusative masculine plural would be 'quos' — 'qui' is nominative plural
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'quibus' — 'qui' is nominative, subject of 'fletis'
hints.dative: Dative would be 'quibus' — 'qui' is nominative plural

translation: Blessed are you who weep now
